skill.name: executing-plans
skill.description: Execute implementation plans with batch processing and review checkpoints. Use when given a plan document.
skill.summary: Guides agents through implementation plans in verified batches with checkpoints.
skill.author: DMJGilbert

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and dismissed as Markdown prose or fenced example text. No executable scripts, shell execution, cryptography, network activity, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Dismissed as Markdown Text
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The external command matches are inline status labels or a fenced Markdown report template, not executable Ruby or shell code. The weak cryptography and network reconnaissance matches are ordinary documentation text with no cryptographic or network operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines contain only front matter, numbered instructions, or a fenced Markdown output template. No executable code or instruction requests network, cryptographic, or shell behavior.

content.user_title: Execute Development Plans in Verified Batches
content.value_statement: Long implementation plans can drift when tasks are completed without checkpoints. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through planned work in small batches with verification before progress continues.
actual_capabilities: Reviews a provided implementation plan before work begins.
Creates a task list from a sound plan.
Executes plan tasks in small batches.
Requires verification before marking tasks complete.
Reports completed tasks, verification results, and issues after each batch.
Stops for user clarification when blockers or unclear instructions appear.
limitations: Requires a separate plan document or clear task list.
Does not create the original implementation plan.
Does not replace project-specific tests or human review.
Does not provide automation scripts or external tool integrations.

use_cases.title: Implement a Feature Plan
use_cases.description: Work through a planned feature in controlled batches with tests after each group of tasks.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Review Checkpoints
use_cases.description: Pause after each completed batch so a reviewer can approve results or redirect the next steps.

use_cases.title: Control High-Risk Changes
use_cases.description: Reduce batch size and stop on blockers when a plan affects important systems.

prompt_templates.title: Run This Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use with a short plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans workflow on this plan. Review it first, create tasks, and complete the first batch only after verification.

prompt_templates.title: Use Smaller Batches
prompt_templates.scenario: Moderate risk work that needs frequent checkpoints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute this implementation plan with a batch size of two tasks. Report completed work, verification results, and blockers after each batch.

prompt_templates.title: Stop on Ambiguity
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this plan for gaps before implementation. Stop and ask questions if any task is unclear or cannot be verified.

prompt_templates.title: Handle High-Risk Changes
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced work touching critical code paths.
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute this high-risk plan one task at a time. Verify each task, report evidence, and wait for approval before continuing.

output_examples.input: Execute a five-task refactor plan with normal risk.
output_examples.output: The assistant reviews the plan, creates a task list, completes the first three tasks, runs the requested checks, and reports pass or fail results before continuing.

output_examples.input: Execute a database migration plan with unclear rollback steps.
output_examples.output: The assistant stops during plan review, explains the missing rollback detail, and asks for clarification before making changes.

output_examples.input: Continue after feedback on the first batch.
output_examples.output: The assistant applies the requested adjustment, updates task status, and proceeds with the next batch using the same verification rules.

best_practices: Provide a concrete plan with tasks, expected checks, and acceptance criteria.
Use smaller batches for risky or unclear changes.
Review each batch report before asking the assistant to continue.
anti_patterns: Using the skill without a plan or task list.
Skipping verification because the change looks simple.
Continuing after blockers without resolving the cause.

faq.question: Does this skill write implementation plans?
faq.answer: No. It is designed to execute an existing plan. Use a planning workflow first if no plan exists.

faq.question: Can I change the batch size?
faq.answer: Yes. The default is three tasks, but the prompt can request smaller or larger batches.

faq.question: What happens when tests fail?
faq.answer: The skill instructs the assistant to stop, report the failure, and avoid marking the task complete.

faq.question: Is it only for coding work?
faq.answer: It is best suited to development plans, but the batch and verification workflow can support similar technical tasks.

faq.question: Does it run commands by itself?
faq.answer: No. The skill describes a workflow. Any command use depends on the assistant, environment, and plan.

faq.question: How does it support review?
faq.answer: It requires a batch report with completed tasks, verification results, issues, and readiness for feedback.
